source_reviews.notes: The release directly reports global tax-and-accounting AI adoption rising to 41%, 35% daily use, and 77% planning higher investment, based on insights from more than 2,700 professionals worldwide. It supports the observed 41% global figure and directional workflow/advisory claims, but not US NAICS-effective adoption, capture, or pricing values; the packet discloses those limits.

CPA.com directly supports workflow automation, augmentation, human-in-the-loop verification, audit transformation, advisory movement, and changing workforce models. It is a qualitative report release rather than a measured CPA-firm task-replacement, adoption-timing, moat, or pricing dataset, and the affected values are presented as bounded estimates.

The 629-respondent PCPS survey directly supports technology/AI change as the leading five-year issue, experienced-staff hiring as the top concern for 11-30-professional firms, and next-generation leadership as the top concern for 31-100-professional firms. It does not measure owner age, consolidator count, transaction multiples, or savings retention; the packet labels those inputs as estimates and expressly discloses the missing censuses and comparables.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 4-digit 5412 roles include non-CPA siblings; actual CPA offices likely have more accountants and fewer clerks.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened realization-rate series; split varies among attest, tax and CAS.
inputs.pi_moat: Source evidence is profession-wide, not a measured small-firm retention rate.
inputs.t50_years: Global survey scope and tool use are not equivalent to NAICS-effective adoption.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed owner-age statistic.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Leadership concern is not a formal universal succession-plan census.
inputs.active_consolidators: No reopened current consolidator census.
inputs.buy_mult: No reopened transaction-comparable series; owner compensation varies.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened small-firm fee survey.
summary: Mechanics and exact artifact bindings pass. All three registered sources were audited and support the core direction on rapid AI adoption, human verification, staffing pressure, and succession concerns. Consolidator, multiple, pricing, owner-age, and capture inputs remain explicitly bounded estimates rather than source-observed values; the 12 authored caveats preserve those limits, and no separate validator-authored caveat or score-changing material defect was found.
